channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump’s foreign aid freeze could prove to be a boon for the world’s authoritarian strongmen
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — When President Donald Trump froze foreign assistance for 90 days, he argued that such a drastic step was needed to eliminate waste and block what he derides as “woke” spending that doesn’t align with American interests.
item.dc:creator: JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bill O'Reilly: Trump will use drones if cartels don't withdraw
item.description: "If they don't recede, you're gonna see dead bodies all over the place and that's coming soon," O'Reilly says.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Elon Musk creates confusion about IRS' Direct File
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Billionaire tech mogul Elon Musk posted Monday on his social media site that he had “deleted” 18F, a government agency that worked on technology projects such as the IRS’ Direct File program.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: California Assembly approves $50M to defend immigrants and protect state policies from Trump plans
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — California's Democratic-dominated Assembly endorsed up to $50 million in funding Monday to defend the state's progressive policies against challenges by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE AUSTIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate tees up final vote on Pam Bondi AG nomination
item.description: The Senate on Monday teed up a final vote on Pam Bondi's nomination to lead the Justice Department. The chamber voted 52-46 to limit debate. Absent an agreement with Democrats, that puts a final confirmation vote on the docket for the early hours of Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Regina Zilbermints, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump and Musk move to dismantle USAID, igniting battle with Democratic lawmakers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration and billionaire ally Elon Musk moved to eradicate the agency that provides crucial aid that funds education and fights starvation, epidemic and poverty overseas, sparking a showdown with congressional Democrats who blasted the effort as illegal and vowed a
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nevada Legislature observes 'family day,' targets high price of eggs as session starts
item.description: State lawmakers convened in Carson City on Monday, beginning the 83rd Legislative Session with the legislative chambers filled with family and friends.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Federal judge issues restraining order blocking federal spending freeze
item.description: The order came in response to a memo released by the Office of Management and Budget last week directing agencies to freeze funding for federal grants and other programs.
item.dc:creator: Elena Moore, MPR News

item.title: Trump agriculture nominee, Brooke Rollins, clears key Senate hurdle ahead of confirmation vote
item.description: Brooke Rollins, the former president and CEO of the America First Policy Institute, had earned broad support from farmers and growers' groups ahead of her Senate confirmation vote.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Speaker’s Lobby: A palpably unfair act
item.description: In football, safeguards exist against subjectively subversive, "palpably unfair" acts. The same can't be said for Washington – much to Trump critics' recent chagrin.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Elon Musk tightens grip on federal government as Democrats raise alarms
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Elon Musk is rapidly consolidating control over large swaths of the federal government with President Donald Trump ’s blessing, sidelining career officials, gaining access to sensitive databases and dismantling a leading source of humanitarian assistance.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NYC mayor downplays economic concerns of Trump deportation plan
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- There have anecdotal reports that immigration raids are driving undocumented New Yorkers into the shadows. New York City has more than 400,000 undocumented workers who help prop up the economy; however, Mayor Eric Adams said he was not worried they would stop working.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Crucial moderate GOP senator backs Tulsi Gabbard ahead of committee vote
item.description: A crucial committee senator said she would vote to advance Tulsi Gabbard to a floor vote in a statement on Monday evening.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Association representing thousands of FBI agents appeals to Congress to protect their jobs
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The association representing thousands of FBI agents urged congressional leaders Monday to protect the jobs of employees at risk of punishment or possibly termination over their participation in investigations into the Jan. 6, 2021, riot at the U.S. Capitol.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump agrees to pause tariffs on Canada in exchange for more border enforcement
item.description: President Donald Trump has agreed to pause tariffs on Canada in exchange for more border enforcement, Prime Minister Justin Trudeau said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: ICE scoops up illegal immigrants with murder, robbery convictions in weekend crackdown
item.description: Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) continues to rack up arrests of convicted criminals as the Trump deportation operation enters its third week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 18 House GOP lawmakers back Trump’s birthright citizenship order in court
item.description: A contingent of House Judiciary Committee Republicans backed President Trump’s order restricting birthright citizenship, filing a brief Monday in a lawsuit filed by four Democratic state attorneys general challenging the order.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Treasury Secretary Bessent, tapped to run CFPB, orders staff to halt work
item.description: Staff at the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau were instructed by email to cease much of their work. Bessent, a wealthy Wall Street investor, replaces Rohit Chopra, who was fired on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Wamsley, MPR News

item.title: Thune on Trump tariffs: 'We’ll see how long' they last
item.description: Senate Majority Leader John Thune (R-S.D.) told reporters Monday he’s taking a wait-and-see approach to President Trump’s order to place 25-percent tariffs on Canada and Mexico and a 10-percent tariff on China, which sent stock markets dropping.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Elon Musk-led DOGE effectively shuts down USAID
item.description: Democratic lawmakers say shutting down the U.S. Agency for International Development is illegal, vowing legal challenges.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: Democratic state Sen. Mallory McMorrow preparing to enter US Senate race in Michigan, AP sources say
item.description: LANSING, Mich. (AP) — Michigan state Sen. Mallory McMorrow is preparing to enter the Democratic primary for an open U.S. Senate seat in the state, multiple people familiar with her plans confirmed on Monday.
item.dc:creator: ISABELLA VOLMERT and JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Multiple Canadian provinces pull US-made booze over Trump tariffs
item.description: At the regional level, provinces are taking their own actions, with multiple premiers saying they have directed their respective liquor boards to stop selling American alcohol.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says he wants Ukraine's rare earth elements as a condition of further support
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Monday indicated that he wants to reach an agreement with Ukraine to gain access to the country's rare earth materials as a condition for continuing U.S. support for its war against Russia.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dozens of Education Department staffers on leave amid Trump's DEI purge
item.description: Dozens of employees with the Department of Education were placed on administrative leave on Friday, allegedly after attending a diversity training course encouraged by then-Secretary Betsy DeVos during the first Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: New York attorney general tells hospitals to continue transgender care after Trump’s executive order
item.description: New York Attorney General Letitia James is telling hospitals that they would be violating state law if they stop offering gender-affirming care for people under age 19 in response to an executive order from President Donald Trump aimed at curtailing federal funding for such treatments.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump agrees to pause tariffs on Mexico, Canada after they pledge to boost border enforcement
item.description: President Donald Trump is holding off on his tariff threats against Mexico and Canada for 30 days after the two U.S. neighbors agreed to boost border security efforts.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Virginia Attorney General asks Costco to end DEI policies
item.description: Virginia Attorney General Jason Miyares is urging the CEO of Costco to end "unlawful" diversity, equity and inclusion policies at the company.
item.dc:creator: Marysa Tuttle, NewsNation

item.title: DeSantis, Florida GOP tensions spill out into open
item.description: Tensions are spilling out into the open between Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis (R) and members of his own party in the Sunshine State, pointing to a new discordant era of GOP-led politics in a state that has trended red over the last few cycles.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, WFLA, NBC

item.title: In immigration crackdown, Trump targets legal pathways
item.description: President Trump campaigned on a pledge to crack down on illegal immigration. But since taking office, he’s primarily targeted several long-standing programs that give migrants lawful ways to come to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Trump fires the director of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau
item.description: President Donald Trump has fired the director of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, Rohit Chopra, in the latest purge of a Biden administration holdover.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: More migrants deported under Biden, Baja state official says
item.description: Tijuana Mayor Ismael Burgueño Ruiz says he will not lift an emergency declaration he made last month as a way to prepare for mass deportations from the United States.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, Fox 5

item.title: Missouri woman pardoned for Jan. 6 offenses sentenced in deadly drunken-driving crash
item.description: Emily Hernandez, a Missouri woman pardoned after her ties to the Jan. 6, 2021, insurrection at the U.S. Capitol, was sentenced Wednesday in connection with a drunken-driving crash.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, Fox 5

item.title: Republican congressman suggests students get jobs to pay for lunches
item.description: Republican Rep. Ritch McCormick (R-Ga.) suggested that students should get jobs to pay for their lunches as the federal funding freeze proposed by President Trump threatens nutrition support for children across the country.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: White House budget office rescinds federal funding freeze memo
item.description: The Trump administration’s Office of Management and Budget released a new memo Wednesday rescinding a controversial memo issued late Monday that froze a wide swath of federal financial assistance, which had paralyzed many federal programs and caused a huge uproar on Capitol Hill.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WFLA, NBC
